title: gnaphalium sepositum
intro: Gnaphalium sepositum, also known as the South African cudweed, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the western and southern parts of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Gnaphalium sepositum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil.
